rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical catalyst-free radical 1,2-alkylarylation of allylic alcohols with malonate-type C–H nucleophiles bearing two or three EWGs, via umpolung of the C–H nucleophile to an electrophilic carbon radical followed by neophyl-type aryl migration.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of the deprotonated malonate carbanion to a tertiary carbon radical; cathode reduces protons to H2.

key_intermediates.name: malonate-derived carbanion (38) from base deprotonation of 2a
tertiary electrophilic carbon radical (39) after anodic SET
benzylic/homoallylic C-radical adduct (40) from radical addition of 39 to allylic alcohol 1a
spirocyclohexadienyl radical (41) from intramolecular aryl ipso-cyclization (neophyl rearrangement)
rearranged tertiary carbon radical / α-hydroxy benzyl-type species (42) after aryl migration
ketone product 3 after electron-proton transfer (oxidation of 42)

electron_flow: Na2CO3 deprotonates the triester 2a to carbanion 38, which is oxidized at the graphite anode (single electron) to tertiary C-radical 39; 39 adds to allylic alcohol 1a giving radical 40, which undergoes ipso-radical cyclization onto the aryl group to spirocyclohexadienyl radical 41, ring-opens via aryl 1,2-migration to 42, and a final anodic electron-proton transfer delivers ketone 3 while the Ni-foam cathode reduces 2H+ to H2.

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of 1a vs 2a
probe_reagents.observation: No oxidation peak for allylic alcohol 1a within the CV window; 2a shows clear oxidation around 1.1 V vs Ag/AgNO3, supporting preferential anodic oxidation of the malonate component.

probe_reagents.name: electron-poor vs electron-rich aryl migration selectivity (products 22–25)
probe_reagents.observation: Preferential migration of more electron-poor aryl groups is consistent with a radical (neophyl-type) rearrangement mechanism rather than a cationic one.

notes: No TEMPO/BHT trapping, KIE, or DFT reported on these pages; mechanistic support rests on CV (selective oxidation of malonate) and the aryl-migration electronic trend characteristic of radical neophyl rearrangement. 2-Arylmalonates failed, suggesting steric/electronic limits on carbanion oxidation or radical addition. Cathodic H2 evolution makes this effectively a paired electrolysis.
